text: An Illinois appellate court found that pro se appellant Robert S. Hulvat filed a brief "produced using artificial intelligence" that cited nine nonexistent cases and fictitious holdings, violating the Illinois Supreme Court AI policy and appellate rules. Hulvat asserted he had used a "well-known generative legal research tool."
ai_system_name: Unnamed generative legal-research tool (per appellant; court finding)
deployer: Pro se plaintiff-appellant Robert S. Hulvat
canonical_source_conflicted: Genuine Illinois case law; Illinois Supreme Court Policy on AI; Rule 375
mitigation_gap: No verification of AI-generated authorities before filing.
reliance_or_harm: Fictitious authorities forced opposing counsel to expend fees; dismissal affirmed.
